design_philosophy.core_loop: Win races by finishing third or higher while engaging in motorcycle combat with opponents; earn money to upgrade bikes and progress through five difficulty levels across five US locations
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Dual-axis challenge: maintain position in race while managing stamina through combat encounters; resource management via bike condition and financial consequences of crashes/fines
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Outlaw biker racing through iconic US landscapes, using street fighting and tactical weapon use to dominate competitors
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Refuses to separate racing from combat—forces constant engagement with melee mechanics during races rather than treating them as optional. Stamina depletion ejects you from bike. Emphasizes consequence through repair bills and police fines that can end runs if player lacks funds. Progression locked behind winning on all five tracks per level before advancement.

difficulty.continues_system: Password system for maintaining progress; game ends prematurely if player cannot afford repair bills or fines
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Bike purchasing decisions create strategic difficulty scaling; players can invest in speed, weight, or steering characteristics. Some bikes feature nitrous oxide for burst advantage. Opponent aggression and course length increase with each level; police frequency and fine costs scale with progression.

progression.upgrade_system: Bike shop allows purchasing motorcycles with different weight/speed/steering profiles; certain bikes include nitrous oxide charges for speed bursts
progression.unlock_mechanism: Must win races (finish third or higher) on all five tracks within each of five levels to advance to next level; higher levels unlock longer courses, increased prize money, and more aggressive opponents
replayability_hooks: Mano a Mano weapon-selection deathmatch mode
Multiple bike options with distinct handling characteristics
Password system enables player choice of when to continue

world_building_techniques: Geographic variation across US locations (Hawaii, Arizona, Tennessee, Alaska, Vermont) creates environmental distinctiveness
Environmental hazards (boulders, cars, deer, cows) provide location-specific racing flavor
Police presence establishes outlaw racing aesthetic

faction_indicators: Opponent racers as rival competitors with escalating aggression
danger_signals: Police officers appear sporadically and can end race participation if player apprehended after crash
Opponent weapons (clubs, chains) can be seized and used against other racers
Financial penalties scale with progression level, creating cascading failure risk
environmental_storytelling: US geographic locations (Hawaii beaches, Arizona desert, Tennessee rural, Alaska wilderness, Vermont mountains) establish road racing subculture across diverse terrain
Traffic obstacles and wildlife hazards reflect realistic highway dangers
Road conditions vary by location, affecting bike handling and crash consequences
